meta.title: WP Sudo — In-Editor Reauthentication Demo
meta.author: dknauss
meta.description: Demonstrates the block-editor (Gutenberg) sudo reauth modal: installing/activating a block from the inserter's Block Directory trips WP Sudo's gate and opens an in-editor password modal that grants a sudo session in place and transparently resumes the request.

steps.code: <?php require_once '/wordpress/wp-load.php'; $mu = defined('WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR') ? WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR : WP_CONTENT_DIR . '/mu-plugins'; if ( is_dir( $mu ) === false ) { mkdir( $mu, 0777, true ); } file_put_contents( $mu . '/wp-sudo-demo-editor-reauth.php', base64_decode('') ); echo 'wrote mu-plugin';

<?php require_once '/wordpress/wp-load.php'; $admin = get_user_by('login', 'admin'); if ($admin) { foreach (array('_wp_sudo_expires','_wp_sudo_token','_wp_sudo_failed_attempts','_wp_sudo_lockout_until','_wp_sudo_failure_event','_wp_sudo_throttle_until') as $key) { delete_user_meta((int) $admin->ID, $key); } } echo 'WP Sudo active; admin arrives with NO sudo session and every fresh editor load starts session-less. Admin password: password. Sudo challenge password: password. Demo: open the block inserter (+), search a Block-Directory block (e.g. "Icon Block" or "Contact Form 7"), and click to install/activate it, then the in-editor sudo password modal appears; enter password to grant a session and the request resumes transparently.';
